items.label: Aronimink Hosts:
items.body: The 2026 PGA Championship is being played at Aronimink Golf Club in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania, marking the club’s first time hosting the PGA Championship.

items.label: Scheffler Defends:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler enters as the defending PGA champion after winning by five shots at Quail Hollow last year.

items.label: McIlroy Momentum:
items.body: Rory McIlroy arrives fresh off his Masters win and is once again near the top of the betting board.

items.label: Career Grand Slam Watch:
items.body: Jordan Spieth can complete the career Grand Slam with a win, adding a strong layer of pressure and intrigue to the week.

items.label: Major Season Pressure:
items.body: This is the second men’s major of 2026, so the field is deep and the margin for error will be tight from the opening round.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+450):
items.body: The defending champion and the betting favorite, with elite form and a tee-to-green profile that gives him the highest baseline of the week.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+800):
items.body: Fresh off a Masters victory and still one of the most dangerous players in the world when the driver is cooperating.

items.label: Cameron Young (+1100):
items.body: A hot hand with wins at The Players and the Cadillac Championship this season, plus enough power to threaten on a long major setup.

items.label: Jon Rahm (+1400):
items.body: A proven major winner with the kind of all-around game that tends to travel well at demanding championship venues.

items.label: Bryson DeChambeau (+1800):
items.body: A high-ceiling power player who can overwhelm courses when his driving is sharp, even if his week-to-week volatility is higher than the top two.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1800):
items.body: A reliable major performer whose balanced game and big-event track record make him one of the safest contenders.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+2000):
items.body: An elite young ball striker whose ceiling rises quickly if the putter gives him any help.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+2200):
items.body: A precision player whose control and approach play fit a stern, major-championship setup.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Aronimink Golf Club in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: May 14 through May 17, 2026.

items.label: Past Champions:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler is the defending champion, with recent PGA winners including Xander Schauffele, Brooks Koepka, and Justin Thomas in the event’s recent history.

items.label: Tournament Status:
items.body: The PGA Championship is golf’s second men’s major of the season and remains one of the sport’s deepest annual tests.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Aronimink Golf Club, Newtown Square, Pennsylvania.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $19 million.

items.label: Winner’s Share:
items.body: $3.42 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-hole stroke play with the full 156-player championship field.

items.label: Thursday:
items.body: 7 a.m.–Noon on ESPN+, Noon–7 p.m. on ESPN, and 7–8 p.m. on ESPN2.

items.label: Friday:
items.body: 7 a.m.–Noon on ESPN+, Noon–7 p.m. on ESPN, and 7–8 p.m. on ESPN2.

items.label: Saturday:
items.body: 8–10 a.m. on ESPN+, 10 a.m.–1 p.m. on ESPN, and 1–7 p.m. on CBS/Paramount+.

items.label: Sunday:
items.body: 8–10 a.m. on ESPN+, 10 a.m.–1 p.m. on ESPN, and 1–7 p.m. on CBS/Paramount+.
